prompt:
Use case: identity-preserve
Asset type: runtime tactics unit sprite
Primary request: remake the Children of the Prophecy Aevum Guardian so it stays compatible with the current faction set but looks noticeably closer to the original concept art
Subject: the same ascetic frontline warrior from the concept, with the broad low conical hat, wrapped face, exposed or lightly wrapped upper torso, simple pilgrim trousers, red waist sash, and long polearm; sparse and martial rather than heavily cloaked
Style/medium: moody post-apocalyptic tactics sprite illustration with painterly pixel-art-inspired rendering, larger readable forms, restrained texture, and clean downscaling at small tactical size
Composition/framing: single full-body subject, centered, grounded stance, transparent background, tight sprite bounds, readable when rendered around 104 px tall on a tactics board
Lighting/mood: solemn dust-filtered dusk light with controlled contrast; spiritual and austere, not flashy
Color palette: sun-bleached bone white, ash beige, muted rust red, worn brown leather, restrained pale gold and very faint ghostly teal accents used sparingly
Constraints: preserve the recognizable original concept silhouette and proportions, especially the broad hat, wrapped face, long polearm, spare clothing, and lean ascetic body language; keep the unit clearly more martial and frontline-oriented than the other Children units; allow only enough cloth layering to keep it compatible with the approved squad palette and rendering language; exactly one subject; no floor; no scenery; no text; no extra subjects; no oversized empty padding
Avoid: heavy cloak mass, bulky satchel loadout, ornate armor, giant VFX, neon magic, anime rendering, glossy sci-fi materials, cinematic background, noisy micro-detail, overly heroic bulk
